Primary request: Create a highly photorealistic close-up construction inspection photograph showing the correct after-final-tightening marking condition of torshear-type high-strength structural bolts.
Scene/backdrop: exposed weathered orange-brown structural steel splice plates at a Japanese steel construction site, with a small glimpse of blue safety mesh near one edge.

Subject: five to seven completed torshear-type high-strength bolt assemblies, each with a large hexagonal nut and round flat washer correctly seated and a short broken pin-tail end. At every fastener show two clearly separated white witness-mark segments: a fixed radial reference line on the steel plate continuing only onto the washer at the 6-o'clock direction, and a white paint segment on one face or edge of the hexagonal nut rotated about 60 to 90 degrees away around the 8-to-10-o'clock direction. The nut-side mark and plate/washer-side mark must not connect or form one straight line. The consistent angular offset proves nut rotation while the fixed reference permits checking for co-rotation. Include realistic scuffed metal, slight surface rust, construction dust, and silver wear.
Style/medium: highly photorealistic documentary construction photography, technically plausible Japanese steelwork inspection, natural imperfect site texture.

Composition/framing: landscape 3:2, tight oblique overhead close-up similar to the photographic reference, with focus centered on at least three complete assemblies where both separated mark segments are unmistakable.
Lighting/mood: strong natural daylight, crisp inspection visibility, realistic hard highlights and modest shadows.

Constraints: final-tightening state only; broken pin tails; all nuts and washers correctly seated; obvious angular discontinuity between every nut mark and its plate/washer reference mark; no continuous aligned mark across plate, washer, and nut; no loose parts, tools, or workers; no labels, printed text, arrows, dimensions, slideshow navigation symbols, logos, trademarks, or watermark.

Avoid: marks aligned in one straight radial line, pre-tightening appearance, unmarked bolts, excessive bolt projection, black pristine studio steel, generic bolt portrait, welds, rebar, schematic illustration, CGI appearance, unsafe or failed connection.
